question_html:
Make sentences with the following group verbs:
‎check in, lay off, pass away, show up, work on.
solution_html:
(i) We must check in at least two hours before the flight.
‎(ii) The company had to lay off several workers due to losses.
‎(iii) Our neighbour sadly passed away last night.
‎(iv) He didn’t show up for the meeting.
‎(v) I am currently working on a new project for my client.

Marks Distribution:
‎(0.5×5) = 2.5 marks for making sentences with group verbs.

question_html:
Write down the correct tag questions of the following sentences:

(a) He hardly knows you, _____

(b) I never told him the truth, _____

(c) Be careful, _____

(d) She is no longer angry, _____

(e) There is a solution to this problem, _____
solution_html:
(a) does he?

(b) did I?

(c) will you?

(d) is she?

(e) isn't there?

Marks Distribution:

(0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks for writing the correct tag questions.

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘Unplanned Civilisation’.
solution_html:
Unplanned Civilisation

Unplanned civilisation refers to the rapid and unstructured growth of cities and human settlements without proper planning or consideration for sustainability. It often results in overcrowded urban areas, poor sanitation, inadequate infrastructure, and environmental degradation. In many developing countries, industrial expansion and population growth have outpaced the capacity of city planning authorities, leading to chaotic traffic, pollution, and insufficient housing. This disorganized growth not only reduces the quality of life but also creates public health hazards, increases crime rates, and strains natural resources. The root causes include rural-to-urban migration, lack of strict building regulations, and weak governance. To address these issues, governments must adopt comprehensive urban planning policies, improve waste management, and ensure sustainable development. Public awareness and community participation are also essential to promote cleaner, greener, and more livable cities. A well-planned civilisation can balance economic progress with environmental care, ensuring a healthier and more harmonious society.

Marks Distribution:

0.5 mark for writing, the Title "Unplanned Civilisation".

02 marks for explaining, how unplanned civilisation causes harm and its bad impacts.

01 mark for writing, causes of the increase in unplanned civilisation.

1.5 marks for writing, measures taken to solve the problems of unplanned civilisation.

[Instructions:

*Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

*0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more spelling mistakes.]
